title: A system for a web platform marketplace of goods and services including a settlement/arbitration algorithm and using blockchain technology
abstract: A system for Web platform marketplace of goods and services, characterized by a novel settlement/arbitration algorithm that increases transactions' fairness and preserves both purchaser's and vendor's rights. Purchaser can acquire credits from platform and use credits to buy goods/service, with reciprocal rating. If purchaser acknowledges he received what agreed, credits are transferred to vendor; otherwise settlement/arbitration process is started and will define the dispute in a short and well defined maximum time. Transactions can be supported by blockchain technology with related advantages. Platform can allow the credits usable for transactions to be converted in a proprietary cryptocurrency.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for a web platform marketplace of goods and services that increases transactionsâ fairness, wherein a transaction includes at least two Parties, wherein the Parties include a Buyer and a Seller, and wherein the system preserves both Buyerâs and Sellerâs rights, wherein Buyer and Seller are associated with respective wallets or accounts, comprising: a) an automated intervention of the settlement/arbitration algorithm in case of disputes, where the algorithm ensures fairness by adhering to preset terms and conditions; b) a settlement algorithm in which, if Buyer considers that he/she has not received, from Seller or on behalf of Seller, the good/service according to the agreed terms, Buyer can initiate a complaint and start the negotiation via the platform, in which Parties explain their motivations and try to solve the dispute by reaching an agreement, wherein an amount corresponding to the good/service according to the agreed terms has not been transferred yet from the Buyerâs wallet or account when the negotiation is started; wherein, according to said settlement algorithm, after a complaint, a Party's failure to respond within the specified timeframe is interpreted by the platform as an implicit acceptance of the opposing Party's position, leading to a swift resolution of disputes; credits will be moved accordingly; c) an arbitration algorithm made accessible to each Party only after the steps of the settlement algorithm have been executed without leading to acceptance, and where human arbiter is guided by the system algorithm. 2. The system according to claim 1 , wherein said settlement algorithm empowers Parties with the ability to renegotiate and reach a settlement that deviates from the initial agreement, offering a flexible framework for dispute resolution, 

wherein the Buyer can accept or reject the Sellerâs standard offer, and the standard offer can be renegotiated between the Parties and become a customized offer and/or a new standard public offer. 3. The system of claim 1 or 2, in which, in case of accepting the offer (standard or customized), the system will check the credit in Buyerâs wallet or account, and will warn the Buyer both via e-mail and platform inbox if the credit amount is lower than said amount, otherwise Parties are informed both via e-mail and platform inbox that the transaction process is started. 4. The system of anyone of the preceding claims, where the Seller informs the platform that service/good was delivered, and automatically the platform emails/inboxes the Buyer that he is required to either: recognize the transaction completion, or start settlement process within a fixed (parametric) time, wherein the platform sends the Buyer automatic scheduled reminders before a set deadline, wherein in case Buyer does not answer within the set deadline, the system considers that Buyer tacitly acknowledges Sellerâs statement and will move credits accordingly. 5. The system of anyone of the preceding claims, in which some or all processes including Settlement/Arbitration algorithm can operate using either traditional digital technology or blockchain technology, based on the administrator's choice, the system further including a human arbitration component that becomes accessible in situations where the automated resolution does not achieve consensus, the human arbitration component operates within the boundaries of the initial agreement and takes into consideration communication and descriptions furnished on the platform by the involved Parties via the settlement algorithm. 6. The system according to any one of claims from 1 to 5, wherein server providing the contract management and settlement and arbitrage service can be connected to a blockchain network with a p2p (peer-to-peer) connection to include the data of the agreement obligation, including the virtual currency and its amount, becoming a method to provide a P2P contract management service using a blockchain-based or non-blockchain-based virtual currency that is stored to be distributed among at least two or more public nodes. 7. The system according to claim 6 that endows involved Parties with the capacity to renegotiate the terms of the transaction, allowing them to reach a settlement that deviates from the initially encoded smart contract agreement. 8. The system of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein (in both cases of traditional or blockchain technology) the web platform provides intuitive user interface tools to facilitate the management and use of credits, their conversion into ERC20 tokens or similar tokens, this conversion increasing the versatility of the system, as these tokens can be traded on the open market, allowing users to take advantage of market fluctuations. 9. The system of any one of claims from 1 to 8, wherein the web platform includes a module for real-time tracking of the conversion rate between the credits and the ERC20 token or similar token. 10. The system of any one claims 1 to 9, wherein the web platform provides a secure digital wallet for each user, facilitating the safe storage, usage, and conversion of credits and ERC20 tokens or similar tokens. 11. The system of any one of claims from 1 to 10, wherein the cryptographic component of the blockchain is utilized to implement ScriptPubKey, a locking script that outlines the unique conditions that must be met for the IITXO to be expended or employed in the settlement/arbitration process. 12. The system of claim 6 that incorporates the Unspent Transaction Output (UTXO) model, which is a model in which each transaction is linked to a distinct UTXO that uniquely signifies a specific quantity of cryptocurrency. 13. The system of claim 6, wherein the ScriptPubKey is configured to mandate a multi-signature requirement, thereby ensuring that a predetermined number of Parties must grant authorization for any arbitration outcome. 14. The system according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein: until a final decision is made by the settlement algorithm and possibly by the arbitration algorithm, the amount corresponding to the good/service according to the agreed terms is not transferred from the Buyerâs wallet or account; when a final decision made by the settlement algorithm and possibly by the arbitration algorithm implies that the Seller has to receive said amount, such amount is transferred from the Buyerâs wallet or account to the Sellerâs wallet.
